original_hypothesis.title: updateFeeRecipient to Non-RON-Receivable Address Permanently Blocks Fee Withdrawal, Inflating Share Price
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: updateFeeRecipient() accepts any address without validating it can receive native RON. If set to a contract that rejects native RON (no receive/fallback), fetchOperatorFee() will always revert in _withdrawRONTo. operatorFeeAmount cannot be withdrawn and grows with each harvest. Since totalAssets() includes operatorFeeAmount in the vault WRON balance, totalAssets is permanently inflated, distorting the share price. New depositors receive fewer shares than fair value, while withdrawers extract inflated value indefinitely.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The feeRecipient can be changed by the owner at any time to any address. There is no validation that the address can accept native RON, which is required by _withdrawRONTo. A malicious or negligent owner could set this to a non-receivable address, creating a permanent accounting distortion.
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: LiquidRon_saigon.s.sol: feeRecipient = 0x00000000000CbA43Ae9379E73c0ccB4E08D7eD30 — needs verification this address can receive native RON

proof_of_signal.pattern: updateFeeRecipient sets feeRecipient = _feeRecipient with no validation; fetchOperatorFee uses _withdrawRONTo which sends native RON via call{value}

preconditions: Owner sets feeRecipient to address that cannot receive native RON

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: No _checkUserCanReceiveRon or similar validation on feeRecipient
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Set feeRecipient to contract without receive() → fetchOperatorFee permanently reverts → operatorFeeAmount inflates totalAssets indefinitely
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Chronic share price inflation benefiting withdrawers at expense of new depositors

economic_questions: What is the long-term effect of permanently stuck operatorFeeAmount on share price?
Can the owner fix this by calling updateFeeRecipient again?
What if the initial feeRecipient becomes non-receivable (e.g., self-destructs)?
validation_requirements: Confirm owner can recover by updating feeRecipient

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: New depositors receiving fewer shares than fair value
potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Proportional to operatorFeeAmount relative to totalAssets

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: feeRecipient address capabilities, operatorFeeAmount growth rate
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Verify revert behavior and owner recovery path

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
